I have concerns about their product authenticity selling in India. Unlike the FDA, supplements, vitamins, and complementary medicines are often considered therapeutic goods, regulated as non-prescription medicines by the TGA. In Australia, they have their own regulated body known as the Therapeutic Goods Administration (TGA). This means that the Swisse company must be compliant with TGA legislation and regulations. When I was searching for this product (High Strength Wild Fish Oil) on their official website, I couldn't find most of their products available in India. I need a clarification; otherwise, it's not good to blindly consume. Swisse/Swisse Wellness Ply Ltd is an Australian-based company.

Turn around and look at the ingredients, and it has omega-6 and omega-9 as well, both of which are pro-inflammatory. Omega-3 is anti-inflammatory, and is beneficial mainly due to that attribute. I have seen plenty of Omega-3-6-9 bottles both in the US and India that have that labeled clearly on the front. This bottle, however, is deliberately misleading. My parents were fooled by this label, and did not find it useful. Cannot be returned because I spotted it after the short return window. That too after they heard of Himalayan Organics' poor reputation. Seems well-deserved.

Questionable ingredients.:  INS codes (International Numbering System for Food Additives) – These codes refer to food additives. Here's a summary of the ones listed:  INS 1201 – Likely a binding or stabilizing agent, generally safe for consumption. INS 468 (Disintegrant) – Sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, used in food and supplements, considered safe. INS 464 – Hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, a thickener, generally safe in food applications. INS 460 – Microcrystalline cellulose, a bulking agent, generally considered safe. INS 470 – Salts of fatty acids, used as emulsifiers, safe for consumption. INS 553 (Glidant) – Magnesium silicate, used to prevent clumping, considered safe in regulated amounts. INS 122 – A synthetic red dye (Carmoisine), which can cause allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. INS 171 and INS 172 – Synthetic food colors (Titanium dioxide and Iron oxide). Titanium dioxide has raised safety concerns, especially in high doses, and is under scrutiny in some countries.

"I’ve always believed in taking care of my body, especially as I’ve gotten older. In my 50s, I started noticing that my energy levels weren’t what they used to be. It seemed like I was always tired, even after a full night of sleep, and I found myself struggling to stay focused at work. My skin was showing signs of aging, too, and I just didn’t feel as vibrant as I once did. It was around this time that I learned about the benefits of resveratrol, particularly for heart health, skin, and cognitive function. After reading some reviews, I decided to try Carbamide Forte Resveratrol capsules. When I started taking them, I wasn’t sure what to expect, but I was hopeful. The first change I noticed was in my energy. I had more pep in my step, and I wasn’t feeling that overwhelming fatigue by mid-day. I started waking up feeling more refreshed, and throughout the day, I felt like I could focus better. This improvement in energy was so refreshing, and it really made me realize just how much I had been lacking.  By week two, I started noticing improvements in my skin as well. It seemed like the dullness was fading, and I could actually see a brighter, more youthful glow. My fine lines appeared to soften, and my complexion looked more even. I hadn’t expected these changes, but I was definitely pleased! I also noticed that my cognitive function was improving—something I really value as I age. My focus at work was better, and I wasn’t as forgetful as I had been.  The most important benefit I noticed, however, was the positive effect on my heart health. I’ve always been careful with my diet and exercise, but after taking Carbamide Forte Resveratrol, I felt an even greater sense of well-being. I felt like I was doing more for my heart by supporting it with such a powerful antioxidant. I started walking more, feeling less out of breath, and overall, my stamina improved.  Carbamide Forte Resveratrol has made a noticeable difference in my health, both physically and mentally. I feel like I’m aging more gracefully, and I’m confident that this supplement is helping me stay healthy. If you’re looking for a way to support your heart, skin, and brain health, this product is definitely worth trying. I’m so happy with the results, and I plan to continue using it long-term!"

About six months ago, I found myself feeling unusually tired and run down. I was struggling with low energy and was constantly feeling fatigued, even after a good night’s sleep. My skin also seemed to have lost its natural glow, and I just didn’t feel like myself anymore. It wasn’t just about physical exhaustion; I noticed my cognitive function wasn’t what it used to be either. I had trouble focusing at work, and I found it hard to stay alert throughout the day. I knew I needed to make some changes, but I wasn’t sure where to start. After doing some research, I came across Carbamide Forte Resveratrol 250mg capsules, which promised to support energy levels, cognitive health, and even heart health. I decided to give it a try, especially because I had read that resveratrol has powerful antioxidant properties. I also liked that it was paired with grape seed extract, which has additional benefits for the cardiovascular system. I was a little skeptical at first, but after just a few weeks of taking these capsules, I began to notice a significant difference. The first change I noticed was my energy levels. I felt more alert, more focused, and less tired during the day. Gone were the afternoon slumps I used to experience, and I could concentrate at work much more easily.  A few weeks later, I started noticing changes in my skin as well. My complexion had been dull for quite some time, and I was beginning to worry about the signs of aging. But after using Carbamide Forte Resveratrol, I saw that my skin started to look brighter and more even. The fine lines around my eyes seemed less noticeable, and I could tell my skin was becoming more youthful and radiant. That was an unexpected bonus, and I couldn’t be happier with the results.  But what really made me stick with this supplement is how much it’s helped my heart health. I’ve always been cautious about maintaining a healthy heart, and resveratrol is known to be great for cardiovascular health. I started feeling more resilient and less winded after walking or exercising. I’ve been incorporating more physical activity into my routine, and I feel confident knowing that I’m doing something positive for my heart.  All in all, I feel like Carbamide Forte Resveratrol has played a major role in improving my overall health. It’s not just about the energy boost or the clearer skin (though those are amazing benefits); it’s about feeling like I’m taking care of my body in a holistic way. I can’t recommend this product enough—it’s become an essential part of my daily routine, and I’m excited to see how much better I can feel as I continue using it. If you’re looking for a natural supplement to support your heart, brain, and skin health, this is the one to try.

I had high hopes when I decided to try Ashwagandha, especially considering all the buzz around its supposed benefits. I was consistent with my intake, taking one capsule a day, and finished the entire bottle. Unfortunately, I can't say I noticed any significant improvements.  In fact, this experience has left me with more doubts than confidence regarding how effective Ashwagandha truly is. After some research, I also realized that many of the claimed benefits of Ashwagandha aren't strongly supported by solid medical evidence. While it might work for some people, it definitely didn't do much for me.  For now, I think I'll be skipping this supplement and looking for alternatives that are backed by more reliable scientific support. If you're considering Ashwagandha, I suggest doing your research and managing your expectations.

Himalayan Organics is a trademark owned by Vlado Sky Enterprise Private Limited. That's all it is. Himalayan Organics doesn't manufacture anything. They source their manufacturing to small Indian companies. This one is made by a little known company called Nexlife Nutrascience (read the label).  This is the most expensive Indian Ashwagandha on Amazon, but it is NOT Ashwagandha root. They have used the whole plant (leaves and stem) to cut cost. It is not 1000 mg per tablet—That claim is misleading. It is 500 mg per tablet and the serving size it 2 tablets. Furthermore, how do you know that this is organic? Do you known where Nexlife Nutrascience source their Ashwagandha from? They could be buying the Ashwagandha powder from a local Ayurvedic pan shop. Who are you going to trust, some Internet seller who claims their product helps with (or is useful/effective for) numerous ailments and is organic without any real proof?  Before using this, or any other, Ashwagandha, you should read about the cases of liver injury with Ashwagandha. This herb is, by itself, hepatotoxic, but if there are contaminants (like heavy metals, pesticides, and bacteria) then it becomes much more insidious.

I'm 33 and live in Delhi. I don't exactly remember since when but it must have been around 18 years since I have been facing the problem of allergic rhinitis. It's not something that troubles me throughout the year but for almost 4-5 months every year, in different seasons, it gets triggered. And when it does, I become extremely sensitive to dust, perfumes or anything having a strong smell. I start sneezing, my eyes get watery and red and I find it very hard to focus and look at my computer screen. I have taken different medications like cetrizine, levocetrizine, allegra, montelucast, fluticasone spray and what not. But all of these medicines only work when I get some sleep after taking them. If I don't sleep, my entire day is ruined with constant sneezing and I can't do any work properly.  Over the years, I have had some good seasons and some bad. There have been some years when my sneezing didn't get triggered at all throughout the year and there have been years when it has been so bad that I have been sneezing from August to December. This year (2020) has been worse. I faced problems in February and March. Then till August I was perfectly fine. But since August, it has been really bad and this month (December) it has been terrible. It's like I have been sneezing almost every day this month. I had given up all hope that anything can work but started doing some research on home remedies. That's when I stumbled upon a blog post which talked about benefits of quercetin supplements. I had nothing to lose so I searched for the same here on Amazon and ordered this product since it had good reviews.  I have been taking this supplement from last 4 days only (2 tablets a day) but surprisingly it has worked well. In fact, this is the only medicine which has worked immediately, without me having to get some sleep after taking it. I haven't found any studies which indicate any adverse effects of this supplement and since it contains pigments which are naturally found in food, I am hoping there should not be much adverse effects and even if there are, I am hoping they should not be as bad as the medicines I had been taking earlier. Also, 4 days is a very short span to comment about the effectiveness of this supplement but I am hopeful it will work in the long term. If it does, it is going to be a life changing medicine for me.  Update (January 5 2021): It's been 2 weeks since I have been taking this. Dosage reduced to 1 tablet per day, sometimes I also skip if I am feeling perfectly fine. So far so good. I hope it continues to work fine

Unlike many other reviewers, I do not take these for asthma or seasonal allergies. I currently take them for headaches, what I call migraine headaches and what my doctor calls tension headaches (we disagree).  I've had problems with headaches for quite a few years now and have always "carefully" used Advil, which usually helped but didn't completely take the headache away. More recently I've been having more headaches and they are powerful bad ones, pain on both sides of the head, tension in the neck and upper back, lasting about 24 hours (+/- 4 hours), and usually include some stomach discomfort such as nausea and lack of appetite. In the past I blamed many of my headaches on caffeine (too much or not enough) but I found those headaches usually went away after a couple hours of sleep. The headaches I get not do not usually diminish with sleep and if I don't take anything for them, the pain prevents me from sleeping.  In the past year I discovered that I was getting headaches every time I consumed pepper - red pepper, chili pepper, cayenne, tabasco, jalapenos, and even red or green bell pepper. Even the tiniest amounts such as contamination would cause one of my severe "migraine" headaches. This has been debilitating as I don't cook much and pepper is in many prepared and restaurant foods, often hidden in ingredients such as "spices" or "natural flavorings", or even just getting in stuff through contamination (same grill, same oil, etc). I grew up heating hot peppers all the time and I love eating at Chipotle's so it's been hugely disappointing to develop this problem. I've found a few other reports online of the same, but it does seem to be a very unusual problem.  Just recently I've discovered that if I take one or two of these as soon as I feel (or suspect) a headache, it seems to stop the headache completely. I do get dry mouth and sometimes urinate a little more than usual as quercetin appears to be a diuretic. Quercetin is also a vasodilator (relaxes or "dilates" blood vessels) as it seems to increase nitric oxide in the blood. Vasodilators are generally considered the cause of migraines, so it seems odd that one vasodilator (peppers) will cause me a headache and another one (quercetin) will take it away. Likely the real mechanisms involved here are more complicated than that. I am pleased though to find that quercetin has this affect as Advil seems to be causing me ulcer-like symptoms (nausea, bloating, acid reflux), even at doses as small as (2) pills every five to seven days.  University of Maryland Medical Center's website says that Quercetin has been known to cause kidney damage at doses greater than 1g (or 1000mg) per day, though I cannot find any scientific study showing that. Each capsule contains 250mg so (4) of these in a day might put you in kidney damage zone. I'm really careful, only taking them when I need them, and I usually try to take only 2 or max 3 in a 24 hour period. I don't think kidney damage can be detected in its early stages so you do want to be careful with these. However, oddly enough, quercetin has also been shown to protect against kidney damage or to help people who have kidney disease, so the studies here seem to be conflicting.  I suspect the headache reducing effect for me will be short lived, or else I'll end up finding out that it also had to do with something else I was doing at the same time as I have been trying other things like cutting out caffeine, exercising, and experimenting with a few other supplements. If I have any more definitive information to add, I'll try to remember to come back and update this review. For now, I am sure that quercetin does take my bad headaches away as I've proven it numerous times (but it does seem to work best if I catch it early).

Quercetin Bromelain was recommended to me some years ago for allergies related to pollens and dust. Living on the Central Coast of California, there is usually something growing that produces pollen. It also seems to work better with consistency.  The pills are a bit large, but have the smooth casing that helps to swallow them. And the price is reasonable compared to other allergy meds. Also, there are NO side effects like sleepiness, jitters, stomach issues.

I developed chronic sinusitis due to a Eustachian tube dysfunction caused by allergies, Not that my nose seemed to be clogged up I didn't notice any clogging in my sinuses but my ears would feel full and my hearing would be muffled. The past few years I would constantly end up at the doctors office to get antibiotics and when my ears would be full they told me I had a dysfunctional Eustachian tube and I had no clue what was causing it. But antihistamines would help subside the symptoms causing me to think it might be inflammation.  Long story short knowing the side effects of taking antibiotics frequently and feeling weighed down from taking allergy pills, even on daytime medication. I started looking for alternate solutions and after reading all the reviews on this product I gave it a try and these past few months have been great. No more sinusitis no more clogged ears. If I feel like my nose is starting to clog up even from a cold, I take the product and my sinus is clear. No more nasal sprays (steroid). Hopefully with pollen season coming up, it will work as well as it did for me during winter season. I am keeping my fingers crossed.  Since this information is not on the bottle I think people should know. After doing some research I found out, people with kidney problems should not use this product. And in some cases taking 1g of bromelain have led to kidney damage. So please use the supplement as directed, or if you use other supplements which include bromelain be careful not to over dose. This will mean if you take this product four times a day, since one dose is 250mg it would be 1g. Also long term usage of bromelain has not been tested so they do recommend taking breaks.

Better than Pseudoephedrine. Works well with my blood pressure medication and I can take as much as I need with hardly any side effect. When my sinus was infected, I used this in larger quatities, about 4 to 6 per dose, twice daily, and I got some minor leg cramps, but with only one of two a day to treat my day-to-day regular sinus problems, I have no side effects whatsoever.  Bromelain is all natrual and is found in Pineapple, the only bromiliad plant that humans can consume with impunity, and is found in meat tenderizers and marinates. It helps to loosen mucous and break it down.  Quercetin shuts down the mucous generator, which is something most mucous medications can't do.  I use this in conjunction with Fexofenedrine and it works pretty well to control my allergies here in Michigan. Many people that live here have the "Michigan Sinus" which is infamous here in Michigan which my doctor told me about. He approved of this regimine with the Quercetin and Bromelain when taken with my anti-allergen of choice. In my opinion, if you have high blood pressure, this is a cheaper, safer alternative than pseudoephedrine.

My MD/ND prescribed Quercetin for me for my excessive throat clearing. It got to the point where my family hated when I woke up in the morning and often through the day. My doctor said to just stay on this and to stop taking antihistamines/decongestants. I had figured maybe I had allergy issues. I don't think that's it. This product has gotten me off those things that can raise blood pressure and feeling clearer than ever. I had brought this up to my regular family MD but he just told me to take antihistamines. I think it took an MD/ND willing to look at me holistically. I have come to the conclusion that traditional medicine can't help people...except if you are cut and bleeding.

This is the only thing thag helped my Fibro joint / muscle plain. I tried everything. Acupuncture, western pain killer, eastern medicine, massages, exercises, nutritionist, every vitamin under the sun, and so on. Nothing helped. While researching Fibro, i heard experts talk about the importance of a healthy gut. One of them mentioned quercetin bromelain. I thought why not. My God. The best leap of faith i have ever taken. I didn’t feel any difference with 2 capsules as suggested on the bottle. I researched on the dosage, and learned people take up to 18 a day. I felt that little extreme. So, i upped my dosage to 4 a day. Immediately, i noticed a difference in my body. Next day, i upped it to 6, and felt like my old self again. I have never written a review on Amazon but felt compelled to do so. I am in the alps right now, and it is really cold so needless my say my joints are little stiff but nothing in comparison to how i used to feel in cold weather. This has been a life saver, and i am so grateful.

I was happy with this product until I was well into my second bottle, and I noticed a cough that I had not had before. It was getting worse. I was not sick, so I thought it must be an allergy, but I wasn't doing anything differently, except take Quercetin with Bromelain. I read up on Bromelin and discovered that if you are allergic to ragweed or pollen, which I am, you might have an allergic reaction. I found this on Mayo Clinic: Allergies: People with other allergies might also be allergic to bromelain. Use cautiously if you are allergic to pineapple, latex, ragweed, Echinacea, chrysanthemums, marigolds, daisies, wheat, celery, papain, carrot, fennel, cypress pollen, grass pollen, or other plants. Upshot - I will take my Quercetin without Bromelain.

This product is truly a miracle. I was skeptical, like anyone purchasing something completely new to me, and I have had asthma for 26 years now. I started the pills the day I received them, but only took two in the evening. I slept great, didn't wake up weezing, just slept clean through the night! Amazing being I was waking up at least twice a night for relief. I thought I'd leave it at just two pills in the evening and see how that went. I ended up feeling a little pressure on my chest during the day, nothing unbearable though. After a couple days I decided to take two more in the morning, that did it! I take 2 pills twice a day, and I don't even think about my inhaler anymore! I have been using these pills for about 3 weeks. I walk/run every day and don't have to worry about taking my inhaler before I go out, nor do I even need one in my purse--just in case. I could not be happier. Now the dosage recommended is 2 pills 3 times a day, but I like to take the smallest dosage necessary, so that's why I didn't just jump into the 6 pills a day. Also, I take this about an hour before I eat, on an empty stomach, and I have had no problems tolerating the pill. I recommend this without reservation. You have nothing to lose--well maybe your inhaler :)

These work better than all the allergy medicines out there today for me. I was SHOCKED at how much better I felt in just a week of using these. I have NO allergy symptoms as compared with using Zyrtec (the best one for me) and still suffering from some mild congestion, fluid in my ears, and occasional burning eyes. With these I never have any symptoms. It took about a week for me to adapt to the switch but 2 months later and I'm still going strong. I have year round allergies to pollen so I needed something that worked better for the peak season.  UPDATE 8/26/14: My mother in law also started using them and after years of allergy suffering using the D version (decongestant) of her allergy meds she has more relief by using the Quercetin!! She also suffers from year round allergies.  I tried switching back to my Zyrtec for a week and ended up with massive fluid in my ears and extreme congestion. Within 36 hours of being back on the Quercetin the congestion was gone and the fluid was receding in my ears. After 4 days all the fluid in my ears was gone again. I am sticking to these no more Zyrtec!

I began using this product after battleing IC for thae past two years. I have taken so many supplements, medications, and teas that I figured what would another hurt. I am convinced my IC is a result of an allergic reaction to my thyroid hormone which I began taking in 2009, exactly six weeks prior to the onset of the IC. No, but I feel better now than I have felt in 2 years. In addition, I have also added a magnesium supplement to my diet. Also, this is the best price I have found anywhere to date.

A friend told me that Quercetin was prescribed to them (by a Naturalist) for improving breathing and reducing allergy symptoms. So I gave it a try. I have been using 1 pill, instead of the 2 that is the recommended dose for this particular Doctor's Best product. I have noticed a significant improvement in my sinuses. I can breathe freer and I have less symptomatic response to allergens. I'm delighted.  Followup, March 2015: I've sometimes been buying Quercetin from the Now Foods brand. I'm now taking the recommended 2 capsules and enjoying the results. As I mentioned, I have reduced reaction to allergens, but the other benefit is that my sinuses drain and "flow" more freely. Even in somewhat dry household conditions, my sinuses usually remain moist and open. I'm less stuffed up in the morning and what is in my head clears out with 1 or 2 noseblows (as opposed to 3 or 4 without quercetin). Bottom line: I make sure I take my quercetin everyday, as the effects are unmistakable and beneficial.

If you've ever has gout, you never want to experience it again. Unfortunately, it's been an ongoing problem for myself. Although I have changed my lifestyle (no pork and very little beef; no alcohol) I still experience flareups. I read about this supplement and had an urge to try it. very glad I did! Experiencing an ongoing flareup which made walking a problem, within a day of taking this, most symptoms had subsided; all symptoms within 2 days. Could it be the Quercetin? Stop taking it and see what happens... gout returns. I took 2 Quercetins and pain subsided in 2 hours! Wow.  Anyone unfortunate to have experience with gout has their own story--what works and what does not. For me, I wish I had found this 10 years ago, but am pleased to know about it now. I take one in the morning w/one Nattokinaise (2000 fu) and repeat the process before dinner. I hope my experience may prove useful to other gout sufferers.

I read about this on Dr. Mercola's website as well as other websites that offer guidance for having a dog with allergies. My rescue didn't come with a history so after a year we pinpointed that her awful scratching, itching, intestinal issue, ear infections and mouth abrasions were not from a food allergy but rather an environmental seasonal allergy. I did not want to give her the antibiotics again because she did not do well with them and they helped only for the ear infection...they didn't stop the allergies. I did two things: I started making her food and there was some improvement but then I started giving her the quercetin. She was a new dog within a week. I am sure the homemade food has improved her health, but this quercetin has given her freedom from constant itching and all the terrible things that came along with it. I will continue to use this for my dog.

I have pretty bad seasonal grass pollen allergy. It was getting worse every year. Allegra stopped working for me, I have a terrible reaction to Claritin, and Zyrtec makes me very sleepy and grumpy. I didn't want to use any steroid sprays. So this year I tried freeze-dried stinging nettles and quercertin. Works great for me! It takes some time to start working, 5-7 days. I tried different doses (1 capsule twice a day, then 1 capsule 3 times a day) and now I take 2 capsules of Dr.'s Best Quercertin/Bromelain (total 500 mg quercertin and 250 bromelain) twice a day. I don't need any anti-histamines at all. I still wouldn't go to a field of pollen-producing grass, and I took 1/4 of 10mg Zyrtec couple of times when pollen counts were high and affected me, but overall I feel great this season!

You would look at it and think that each capsule has 892mg epa and 594mg dha, however, that's not the case, each capsule actually has only 446mg epa and 297mg dha, that's because this company has used the clever trick of having its serving size be 2 capsules, instead of 1 capsule.  Now, despite the false advertising, the product itself is still fine, it has less fishy burps and still gives you good value for money, but it is only half as good as it is claimed.  The mention of the serving size being 2 capsules is also absent from their test report photo , and is only visible on the bottle itself (which they are legally required to put there). I have also attached photo of both their test report and the bottle itself.  Imo, the wow triple strength fish oil is better, but it's upto you in the end, be sure to research before buying :)

As a doctor, I have myself tried & suggested to my patients , more than 1 dozen of Curcumin Brands over the last 10 years of Medical practice.  But this Rasayanam one is the worst Curcumin, I have ever seen. Do not try to open the capsule and take curcumin powder directly on tongue or chew the curcumin capsule... because u may feel nausea or even vomit within no time.  The bottle carries green mark to show that this product is vegetarian or vegan but curcumin powder is itself contaminated with something that loudly speaks that it is no longer vegetarian or vegan.  Further Capsules are declared as "Natural capsules" to hide the Gelatin content. I am 100% sure they contain Gelatin and cheapest Gelatin, which can be identified by keeping the capsule in mouth cavity for 8-12 seconds.  When I called Rasayanam customer service, to complain about their curcumin, they do not pick the phone even after 2 or more attempts.  When I tried to chat on whatsapp, they just keep sending Sorry messages and do not give their calling number and do not call even after request for calling.  I have seen the behaviour of this company earlier also when I purchased their multivitamin. They do not give info about the blue colour coating. After continuously asking, they given false information about the colour and told that it is Titanium dioxide ... Friends ! Titanium dioxide is white in colour. Clearly they are hiding the things.  I also ordered their KSM Ashwagandha and I told them their Ashwagandha is containing solid fine wood strands embedded within the Ashwagandha matter, they asked for photographs. When I shared the photographs, they asked for parcel and product opening video, which I didn't make. They were not ready to accept their shortcomings and negative points.  Once again the same fooling & cheating behaviour !  Finally I feel that this company and administration is only money minded and does not care about their customers and consumers. Irresponsible behaviour ! Irresponsible company !  So think before purchasing Curcumin, Multivitamin and Ashwagandha of Rasayansm...  You may also regret for your purchase like me.

I bought this because the front label loudly highlights “1600 mg Turmeric Curcumin.” Like most buyers, I assumed that meant a strong curcumin dose. After receiving it and reading the composition: Standardized Curcumin (95%) = 100 mg per capsule That’s a very different picture. To be clear, I’m not saying the ingredients are bad. Turmeric and piperine are fine additions. But the way the dosage is presented on the packaging can easily give the impression of a much higher active curcumin content than what’s actually standardized. For customers specifically looking for higher-dose curcumin, this distinction really matters. What I liked: ✔ Decent packaging ✔ Includes piperine ✔ No noticeable side effects What disappointed me: ✘ Front-label emphasis feels confusing ✘ Only 100 mg standardized curcumin per capsule ✘ Expectations didn’t match what I understood from the box Final thought: Please read the composition carefully. If you’re buying mainly for standardized curcumin strength, you may want to compare labels before deciding.
